name: Ninja Foodi 14-in-1 8-Qt. XL Pressure Cooker Steam Fryer

specs.Cooking Functions: 14 — Pressure, Air Fry, Steam, Slow Cook, Sear/Sauté, Bake, Roast, Broil, Dehydrate, Sous Vide, Yogurt, Steam Crisp, Proof, Keep Warm
specs.TenderCrisp Technology: Pressure cook then air-crisp in same pot

whatWorks: TenderCrisp technology is the standout feature — you pressure cook ribs, chicken, or pot roast until tender, then swap lids and air crisp the exterior to a proper crust, all in the same pot without transferring food.
The 8-Qt capacity fits a whole chicken, a rack of ribs, or enough chili for 8 people, making it genuinely useful for large households where smaller multi-cookers fall short.
14 cooking functions cover pressure cooking, slow cooking, air frying, baking, dehydrating, and sous vide — replacing a pressure cooker, slow cooker, air fryer, and dehydrator in one appliance.

Owners consistently report better air fry results than they expected from a multi-cooker, especially on proteins where the combination of pressure-cooked moisture retention and air-crisped exterior is hard to replicate with a standalone device.
worthKnowing: The machine is large and heavy — at 8Qt it takes up significant counter space and is difficult to move once placed, which is a real consideration in smaller Canadian kitchens.
Two separate lids (pressure lid and crisping lid) add complexity — the swap mid-cook is intuitive once learned, but the first few uses require keeping the manual nearby.
At $349 CAD, it's significantly pricier than a standard Instant Pot and harder to justify if you don't actively use the TenderCrisp function.
Air fry results, while good for a multi-cooker, are slightly below a dedicated air fryer — surface crispness on things like french fries is marginally less even.

commonComplaints.title: Takes up my entire counter and…
commonComplaints.body: Takes up my entire counter and is too heavy to move in and out of a cabinet. You have to commit to permanent counter space.

commonComplaints.title: Two lids are awkward — where do…
commonComplaints.body: Two lids are awkward. Where do you put the pressure lid when you're using the crisping lid? I had nowhere to set it safely.

commonComplaints.title: Air fry is not as crispy as…
commonComplaints.body: Air fry results are good but not as crispy as my dedicated air fryer. For proteins it's great; for fries it's just okay.

commonComplaints.title: Price is hard to swallow for…
commonComplaints.body: Price is hard to swallow. I love it but I had to think hard about spending $350 on one pot.

verdict: The Ninja Foodi 14-in-1 earns its price for households that will actually use TenderCrisp — the ability to pressure cook and air crisp in one vessel without transferring food is a genuine convenience that standalone appliances can't replicate. The two-lid system is more manageable than it sounds after a few uses. If you want the best multi-cooker for proteins and roasts in Canada, this is the pick; if you rarely air fry and mainly pressure cook, an Instant Pot Pro at half the price covers most of the same ground.

realBuyers.quote: Made ribs in under two hours — pressure cooked them tender then crisped the outside. Better than anything I've made on the grill.
The TenderCrisp function on a whole chicken is unreal. Juicy inside, crispy skin, done in 45 minutes.
Replaced my Instant Pot, air fryer, and slow cooker. Counter is finally clear.
8Qt is the real selling point. My 6Qt Instant Pot couldn't fit a rack of ribs. This one does with room to spare.

sources.title: Ninja Foodi OL701 Review — Serious Eats

metaDescription: Ninja Foodi 14-in-1 8-Qt review for Canada: pressure cook and air crisp in one pot with TenderCrisp technology. Honest on size, dual lids, and price. ~$349 CAD.
introText: The Ninja Foodi 14-in-1 combines pressure cooking and air frying in an 8-Qt pot, with TenderCrisp technology that lets you pressure cook and then crisp food in the same vessel. It's the most versatile multi-cooker available in Canada and available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B08C3NTSHK) at around $349 CAD.
